content.problemStatement:
A string with length LLL is called rich if L≥3L \ge 3L≥3 and there is a character which occurs in this string strictly more than L/2L/2L/2 times.

You are given a string SSS and you should answer QQQ queries on this string. In each query, you are given a substring SL,SL+1,…,SRS_L, S_{L+1}, \ldots, S_RSL​,SL+1​,…,SR​. Consider all substrings of this substring. You have to determine whether at least one of them is rich.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if the given substring contains a rich substring or `"NO"` if it does not contain any rich substring.
